raw_consumer_statement: I hired a freelancer on XXXX to deliver a monetized XXXX channel for {$7300.00}, paying {$3600.00} upfront via American Express on [ insert date ]. In clear XXXX messages, the freelancer promised delivery in a few hours after I released the {$3600.00} from escrow ( screenshots attached ). They also signed an agreement stating theyd issue a 100 % refund at any stage, no matter what. I released the funds expecting quick delivery, but no channel was provided. Soon after, XXXX banned the freelancer for a policy violation and closed the project, suggesting fraud. XXXX refused a refund, saying released funds are final, despite their ban preventing delivery. I filed a chargeback with Amex for services not rendered, citing the promise, non-delivery, and refund clause. Amex gave me a temporary {$3600.00} credit but later sided with XXXX, claiming I authorized the payment. My appealshowing the few hours promise, refund agreement, and XXXX banwas denied. Amex ignored evidence the freelancer couldnt deliver due to XXXX actions and breached their own refund promise. This violates my rights under the Fair Credit Billing Act ( 15 U.S.C. 1666 ) to dispute charges for undelivered services. Now, XXXX demands I repay {$3600.00} for the chargeback, threatening collections, while Amex holds me liable for a fraudulent transaction they shouldve reversed.
